intro: Chapmannia prismatica, also known as the "Sudan thistle," is a spiny, thistle-like plant native to Africa that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Chapmannia prismatica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chapmannia prismatica include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
